scenario_summary: A security fix authored on the stable train is backported to the LTS train via backport_lane.stable_to_lts. The change is admissible because security_fix is in admissible_change_classes; new_feature_invention, public_api_widening, schema_widening, and support_window_widening would have been refused. The same change set MUST update both the claim_docs_known_limit_bundle and the mirror_and_offline_bundle because LTS rows admit offline_signed_bundle and customer_managed_mirror publication postures.

explanation_fields.user_facing_summary: A security fix from stable has been backported to the LTS line. Your settings, recovery state, and managed policy are unaffected because every LTS state root is channel-suffixed.
explanation_fields.release_notes_summary: Backport from stable to LTS is admissible only for security fixes, data-loss / trust corrections, bug fixes, and docs / help corrections. The same change set MUST update the claim / docs / known-limit bundle and the mirror / offline bundle.
explanation_fields.support_summary: Support exports cite the backport_lane.stable_to_lts row, the security_fix change class, the LTS support window, and the mirror / offline manifest revision so reviewers can confirm the backport scope is bounded by the LTS support window.
explanation_fields.promotion_summary: Promote. The backport carries one named correction scope; no widening on the LTS train. The claim / docs / known-limit and mirror / offline bundles move in the same change.

notes: Reference fixture for the backport_lane.stable_to_lts patch lane.
